verses.text: The disciples pluck corn on the sabbath, and the Pharisees object. Petty zeal. They strain at a custom while missing the heart of the law.
The disciples pluck corn on the sabbath, and the Pharisees object. Petty zeal. They strain at a custom while missing the heart of the law.
He cites David eating the shewbread, and the priests' sabbath work in the temple. Precedent. Scripture itself allows mercy and necessity on the sabbath.
He cites David eating the shewbread, and the priests' sabbath work in the temple. Precedent. Scripture itself allows mercy and necessity on the sabbath.
He cites David eating the shewbread, and the priests' sabbath work in the temple. Precedent. Scripture itself allows mercy and necessity on the sabbath.
'I will have mercy, and not sacrifice'; the Son of man is 'Lord even of the sabbath day.' Mercy over form. The day was made for man's good, and its Lord is Christ.
'I will have mercy, and not sacrifice'; the Son of man is 'Lord even of the sabbath day.' Mercy over form. The day was made for man's good, and its Lord is Christ.
'I will have mercy, and not sacrifice'; the Son of man is 'Lord even of the sabbath day.' Mercy over form. The day was made for man's good, and its Lord is Christ.
He heals a withered hand, asking if it is lawful to do good on the sabbath; a man would rescue a sheep from a pit. Good is lawful. Doing kindness never breaks God's day.

The Pharisees plot to destroy Him; He withdraws and heals many, charging silence. Meek retreat. He does not seek conflict, but quietly continues His work of mercy.
The Pharisees plot to destroy Him; He withdraws and heals many, charging silence. Meek retreat. He does not seek conflict, but quietly continues His work of mercy.
The Pharisees plot to destroy Him; He withdraws and heals many, charging silence. Meek retreat. He does not seek conflict, but quietly continues His work of mercy.
This fulfils Isaiah: 'a bruised reed shall he not break'; He shall bring forth judgment to the Gentiles. Gentle Servant. The Saviour deals tenderly with the weak and bruised.
This fulfils Isaiah: 'a bruised reed shall he not break'; He shall bring forth judgment to the Gentiles. Gentle Servant. The Saviour deals tenderly with the weak and bruised.

He heals a blind and dumb demoniac; the Pharisees say He casts out devils 'by Beelzebub.' Vile slander. They credit the work of God to the devil.
He heals a blind and dumb demoniac; the Pharisees say He casts out devils 'by Beelzebub.' Vile slander. They credit the work of God to the devil.
He heals a blind and dumb demoniac; the Pharisees say He casts out devils 'by Beelzebub.' Vile slander. They credit the work of God to the devil.
A kingdom divided cannot stand; if He casts out devils by God's Spirit, 'the kingdom of God is come unto you.' Sound logic. His power over demons proves God's reign has arrived.
A kingdom divided cannot stand; if He casts out devils by God's Spirit, 'the kingdom of God is come unto you.' Sound logic. His power over demons proves God's reign has arrived.

A kingdom divided cannot stand; if He casts out devils by God's Spirit, 'the kingdom of God is come unto you.' Sound logic. His power over demons proves God's reign has arrived.
None spoils a strong man's house unless he first binds him; 'he that is not with me is against me.' No neutrality. There is no middle ground in the war between Christ and Satan.
None spoils a strong man's house unless he first binds him; 'he that is not with me is against me.' No neutrality. There is no middle ground in the war between Christ and Satan.
All sin may be forgiven, but 'the blasphemy against the Holy Ghost' shall not, in this world or the next. A solemn warning. To call the Spirit's work devilish is to harden past pardon.
All sin may be forgiven, but 'the blasphemy against the Holy Ghost' shall not, in this world or the next. A solemn warning. To call the Spirit's work devilish is to harden past pardon.
The tree is known by its fruit; 'out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh.' Words reveal. Speech is the overflow of what fills the heart.
The tree is known by its fruit; 'out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh.' Words reveal. Speech is the overflow of what fills the heart.
The tree is known by its fruit; 'out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh.' Words reveal. Speech is the overflow of what fills the heart.
Men shall account for every idle word; 'by thy words thou shalt be justified, and... condemned.' Weighty speech. God takes notice of our careless words.
Men shall account for every idle word; 'by thy words thou shalt be justified, and... condemned.' Weighty speech. God takes notice of our careless words.
They seek a sign; none is given but that of Jonas — three days in the whale, as the Son of man in the earth. The great sign. His death and resurrection are the proof above all others.
They seek a sign; none is given but that of Jonas — three days in the whale, as the Son of man in the earth. The great sign. His death and resurrection are the proof above all others.
They seek a sign; none is given but that of Jonas — three days in the whale, as the Son of man in the earth. The great sign. His death and resurrection are the proof above all others.
Nineveh and the queen of the south shall condemn this generation, for 'a greater than Jonas... than Solomon is here.' Greater light. To slight Christ is worse than the heathen's ignorance.
Nineveh and the queen of the south shall condemn this generation, for 'a greater than Jonas... than Solomon is here.' Greater light. To slight Christ is worse than the heathen's ignorance.
An unclean spirit returns with seven worse; so this generation's last state is worse than the first. Empty reform. A heart swept but not filled with God is open to deeper evil.
An unclean spirit returns with seven worse; so this generation's last state is worse than the first. Empty reform. A heart swept but not filled with God is open to deeper evil.

Told His mother and brethren wait, He says, 'whosoever shall do the will of my Father... is my brother, and sister, and mother.' Family of faith. Obedience to God forms the closest of bonds with Christ.
